response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but the fit with this specific role is weak. The position mandates 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows, strong SQL Server/Oracle experience, and fixed-income or capital-markets domain knowledge. The candidate's C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a thesis project, his database background is MongoDB/MySQL rather than SQL Server/Oracle, and his financial experience (RIDE Capital) is fintech product work in Node/TypeScript, not capital markets or fixed income. His depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which does not satisfy the mandatory requirements.
strengths: Broad, senior-level engineering experience across web, backend, and low-latency systems (DLang, async I/O at Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, open-source contributions
Some systems-level C/C++ background (National Instruments LabWindows/CVI, C++ audio thesis project)
concerns: Far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of professional C++ on Windows — only ~6 months of relevant C/C++ work, a decade ago
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience; RIDE Capital was retail-investment fintech in a different stack

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering seniority and fundamentals
